etymology.description: Depicts a weaving tool, a carrying pole, or a double-bladed axe
etymology.interpretation: The character 壬(rén, "ninth heavenly stem") depicts a physical object, though its precise identity is uncertain. Proposed interpretations include a spool for weaving, reflecting its potential connection to other tools, or a carrying pole, suggesting it as an original form of 任(rèn, "to bear"). Additionally, it may represent a double-bladed axe, reminiscent of its visible form.
